message:
feat(abi): eliminate glibc call-through for ~25 unistd/POSIX symbols with native and raw-syscall implementations

Replace GlibcCallThrough with deterministic native implementations for a
large batch of unistd functions, removing glibc runtime dependencies and
adding full membrane runtime-policy gating to each entrypoint.

Newly native implementations:
- confstr: const value table (currently _CS_PATH) with POSIX copy/length contract
- pathconf/fpathconf: value table with stat/fstat validation and POSIX errno contract
- getpagesize: delegates to existing sysconf table
- gethostid: deterministic FNV-1a hash of uname nodename (no glibc, no /etc/hostid)
- getdomainname: SYS_uname-backed extraction with Linux-compatible truncation
- ctermid: constant /dev/tty path with static fallback buffer
- sethostname/setdomainname: raw SYS_sethostname/SYS_setdomainname with policy gating
- get_nprocs/get_nprocs_conf: sysconf-backed with positive fallback
- get_phys_pages/get_avphys_pages: SYS_sysinfo-backed with saturating page normalization
- lockf: full ULOCK/LOCK/TLOCK/TEST via fcntl F_SETLK/F_SETLKW/F_GETLK
- posix_fallocate: new sys_fallocate raw syscall (x86_64: 285, aarch64: 47)
- posix_madvise: delegates to sys_madvise with POSIX error-code-return contract

Refactoring:
- Extract read_utsname() and uts_field_len() helpers, refactor gethostname to use them
- Replace out.iter().any(|&b| b == 0) with idiomatic out.contains(&0) in wchar realpath

Testing: 398 new lines of tests covering lockf roundtrip, posix_fallocate negative
ranges, posix_madvise errno preservation, confstr value/length, pathconf/fpathconf
validation, getpagesize consistency, getdomainname truncation, gethostid determinism,
ctermid null/non-null, sethostname/setdomainname EPERM, get_nprocs/get_phys_pages
positivity, sysconf known-names, and all LP64 alias functions (open64, creat64,
stat64, lstat64, fstat64, fstatat64, lseek64, truncate64, ftruncate64, pread64, pwrite64).

Support matrix: ~25 symbols updated from GlibcCallThrough to Implemented or RawSyscall
with detailed strict/hardened semantics documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
